Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a single gaming product or redeem code available for sale on Castro Nepal.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The name of the product, e.g., 'PlayStation Store Gift Card $50'.

shortDescription.description: A brief summary of the product, suitable for display on product cards.

fullDescription.description: The comprehensive description of the product, displayed on the product details page.

price.description: The price of the product in the local currency.

imageUrl.description: The URL of the main image for the product.

categoryIds.description: References to Categories this product belongs to. (Relationship: Category N:N Product)

stockStatus.description: The current availability status of the product (e.g., 'In Stock', 'Out of Stock', 'Low Stock').

isFeatured.description: A flag indicating if the product should be prominently displayed as a featured item, typically on the homepage.

isTrending.description: A flag indicating if the product is currently trending or highly popular among users.

customerInstructions.description: Specific instructions for the customer regarding the use or redemption of the digital product code.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the product was first added to the catalog.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the product information was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Organizes products into logical groups like 'PlayStation Redeem Codes' or 'Xbox Gift Cards'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the product category, e.g., 'PlayStation Redeem Codes'.

description.description: A short description of the category, explaining its purpose or content.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores user-specific information for registered customers, excluding authentication credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, corresponding to the user's account.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and manual login (managed by external auth).

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

address.description: The user's primary shipping or billing address for saved contact information.

googleId.description: An identifier provided by the Google authentication system if the user signed up via Google. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 ExternalAuthSystem)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's purchase transaction on the website.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who placed the order. Null if the order was placed as a guest. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Order)

customerName.description: The full name of the customer who placed the order.

customerPhoneNumber.description: The phone number of the customer for order communication.

customerAddress.description: The physical address provided by the customer for the order.

customerEmail.description: The email address of the customer for order confirmation and communication.

notes.description: Any additional notes or messages provided by the customer with the order.

orderTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including all items and any applicable taxes/fees.

status.description: The current processing status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Processing', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a specific product and its quantity within a larger customer order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product that was ordered. (Relationship: Product 1:N OrderItem)

productName.description: The name of the product at the time of purchase (denormalized for historical accuracy).

priceAtPurchase.description: The price of the product at the time it was purchased (denormalized for historical accuracy).

quantity.description: The quantity of this specific product ordered.

Announcement.title: Announcement
Announcement.description: Stores information about system-wide announcements created by administrators.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Announcement entity.

content.description: The main text content of the announcement.

startDate.description: The date and time when the announcement becomes active and visible.

endDate.description: The date and time when the announcement expires and is no longer visible (optional).

isActive.description: A boolean indicating if the announcement is currently active and should be displayed on the homepage.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the announcement was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the announcement was last updated.

definition.description: Stores all gaming products and redeem codes available for sale. These products are publicly readable by all users (authenticated or not) and writable only by administrators. Includes denormalized 'isFeatured' and 'isTrending' fields to support efficient listing queries for homepage sections, directly addressing QAPs for these public lists.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific product.

definition.description: Contains categories for organizing products. Categories are publicly readable for navigation and filtering, and writable only by administrators. This collection supports efficient QAPs for category lists.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific product category.

definition.description: Stores user-specific profile information. Access is restricted to the owner of the profile (request.auth.uid == userId) for read/write operations. This path adheres to path-based ownership for authorization independence, ensuring users can only manage their own data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores customer purchase transactions. Each order document includes 'userId' (if placed by a registered user) or is null for guest orders, allowing authorization independence for individual user orders (request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId). Administrators have full read/write access to all orders. This structure enables QAPs for users to list their own orders efficiently via a query filter, and for admins to list all orders.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific customer order.

definition.description: Represents individual products within an order. Each OrderItem document denormalizes the 'userId' from its parent Order for authorization independence. Access is granted if 'request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId' or if the user is an administrator. This ensures that access rules can be applied directly to OrderItem documents without requiring a 'get()' call to the parent Order, supporting QAPs for viewing order details.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order to which this item belongs.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific item within an order.

definition.description: Manages system-wide announcements displayed on the homepage. Announcements are publicly readable by all users and writable only by administrators. This structure provides a homogeneous security posture for efficient public listing (QAP).

params.name: announcementId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific announcement.

definition.description: This collection serves as the database-backed access control (DBAC) for administrators. The mere existence of a document at '/roles_admin/{userId}' grants administrative privileges to that 'userId'. This provides a secure and auditable way to manage administrator roles without relying on custom claims, supporting global role checks for write access on public collections and full access on sensitive collections (e.g., all orders).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier (UID) of a user who has been assigned administrator privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Castro Nepal is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence and supporting Query Across Paths (QAPs) to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**

1.  **Public Collections (`/products`, `/categories`, `/announcements`):** These collections store data intended for public read access by all users (authenticated or not). Their security rules are simple (`allow read: if true;`) as there are no inherent hierarchical authorization dependencies for read operations. Write access is strictly limited to administrators, checked via the dedicated `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection. This eliminates any need for `get()` calls in read rules.
2.  **User-Owned Data (`/userProfiles/{userId}`):** User profiles are designed using path-based ownership (`/userProfiles/{userId}`). Authorization is directly tied to the document path, where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. This provides absolute authorization independence, as no `get()` calls are required to verify ownership. A user can only access their own profile.
3.  **Transactional Data (`/orders/{orderId}` and `/orders/{orderId}/orderItems/{orderItemId}`):**
    *   **Orders:** The `/orders/{orderId}` collection is a top-level collection. Each `Order` document explicitly includes a `userId` field (or `null` for guest orders). For authenticated users, read/write access to an order is granted only if `request.auth.uid` matches the `resource.data.userId` within the order document. This denormalizes the authorization context directly into the document, avoiding any `get()` calls to a parent `UserProfile` or similar. Administrators have full access, checked by `/roles_admin/{userId}`.
    *   **OrderItems:** `OrderItem` documents are stored in a subcollection under `/orders/{orderId}/orderItems/{orderItemId}`. Crucially, each `OrderItem` document also denormalizes the `userId` from its parent `Order` into its own fields. This ensures that security rules for `OrderItem` can independently verify ownership (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId`) without requiring a `get()` call to the parent `Order` document, adhering strictly to the Authorization Independence mandate.

**Query Across Paths (QAPs) Support:**

1.  **Public Lists (`/products`, `/categories`, `/announcements`):** These collections are designed with a homogeneous security posture (publicly readable). This enables efficient `list` operations (QAPs) across the entire collection without security rule filters. Users can query for featured products, trending items, or filter by category directly, and the rules `allow list: if true;` are performant and secure.
2.  **User-Specific Lists (`/orders`):** For logged-in users, `list` operations on `/orders` are secured by requiring a query filter `where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. The security rules will enforce that `request.auth.uid` matches the `userId` field in the documents being requested. This ensures that users can only list their own orders, directly supporting QAPs for personal order history. Administrators, identified via `/roles_admin`, can list all orders.
3.  **Admin Roles (`/roles_admin/{userId}`):** This dedicated collection serves as a database-backed access control (DBAC) mechanism. The mere existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{userId}` (where `userId` is `request.auth.uid`) grants administrative privileges. This design avoids custom claims, simplifies rule logic (e.g., `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`), and provides a clean, auditable record of administrators, critical for securing write operations across public collections and granting full access to sensitive collections like `orders` and `userProfiles` for administrative oversight.
